settings.title: The Key Workers Who Know Your Child
settings.excerpt: A look at the people who greet your child each morning and the small things they quietly keep track of.
settings.body:
When you leave your child with us, you are really trusting one particular person. At Little Oaks that is their key worker, and we want parents to know who they are.

Each key worker looks after a small group, learning their favourite games, their comfort routines, and the words they are just starting to say. They are the ones who notice when a quiet child has had a brilliant day.

What a key worker does

The role is less about a job description and more about knowing a child well. A key worker is the steady, familiar face in your child's day, and that constancy is what lets a little one feel safe enough to explore. In practice they:

  • Greet your child each morning and help them settle.
  • Keep track of how they are growing and what they are ready for next.
  • Are your first point of contact for anything, big or small.

Our key worker Sofia keeps a little notebook for every child in her group. She says the joy of the job is watching a shy toddler in September turn into a confident chatterbox by summer.

Talk to them

If anything is on your mind, your child's key worker is the person to ask. They know your little one as well as anyone here, and a quick word at pick up often sorts out a worry before it grows. We would always rather you asked than wondered.
